skill.name: stripe-best-practices
skill.description: Guides Stripe integration decisions — API selection (Checkout Sessions vs PaymentIntents), Connect platform setup (Accounts v2, controller properties), billing/subscriptions, Treasury financial accounts, integration surfaces (Checkout, Payment Element), migrating from deprecated Stripe APIs, and security best practices (API key management, restricted keys, webhooks, OAuth). Use when building, modifying, or reviewing any Stripe integration — including accepting payments, building marketplaces, integrating Stripe, processing payments, setting up subscriptions, creating connected accounts, or implementing secure key handling.
skill.summary: Official Stripe best-practices reference for API selection, Connect, billing, tax, Treasury, and secure key management.
skill.author: stripe

security_audit.summary: This is a pure documentation skill from Stripe's official AI repository containing 7 markdown reference files with no executable code. All 167 static findings are false positives: backticks in code examples were misidentified as shell execution, docs.stripe.com URLs flagged as hardcoded URLs, 'SAML' in SSO section matched 'SAM' database pattern, and documentation prose triggered generic 'reconnaissance' and 'crypto' heuristics. No malicious intent or dangerous behavior was found.

low_findings.title: False positive: Ruby/shell backtick execution detected in markdown documentation
low_findings.description: The static scanner flagged 79 instances of 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' across the skill files. After review, all are false positives: the backticks appear in markdown code fences, inline code formatting, and JavaScript template literal examples (e.g., `${url}/success?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}` in billing.md). No actual command execution is present in any file.

low_findings.title: False positive: Hardcoded URL detection on documentation links
low_findings.description: 63 instances of 'Hardcoded URL' were flagged. All URLs in this skill point to docs.stripe.com, dashboard.stripe.com, or support.stripe.com — official Stripe documentation. These are reference links in a documentation skill, not network calls or data exfiltration vectors.

low_findings.title: False positive: 'Windows SAM database' matched 'SAML' in SSO documentation
low_findings.description: The scanner flagged 'Windows SAM database' at security.md:14, :105, and :107. These are false positives: line 105 is the 'SAML and SCIM' section header (an SSO protocol), line 14 is a table of contents reference to SAML, and line 107 discusses SSO via SAML. No Windows registry or credential store access is present.

low_findings.title: False positive: Weak cryptographic algorithm detection
low_findings.description: Several 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings were triggered by words like 'signing' (webhook signatures) and 'hash' in the security documentation. The skill actually recommends strong cryptographic practices: webhook signature verification using Stripe's signing secret and SAML-based SSO.

low_findings.title: False positive: System reconnaissance detected in security guidance
low_findings.description: Lines flagged as 'System reconnaissance' (e.g., billing.md:7, tax.md:21) are recommendations to 'check' supported regions, 'review' logs, or 'audit' code — standard security best-practice language, not actual reconnaissance commands.

content.user_title: Build Stripe integrations the right way
content.value_statement: Choosing between Checkout Sessions, PaymentIntents, and Connect can be confusing and costly to fix later. This skill provides Stripe's official guidance to help you select the correct API, avoid deprecated endpoints, and implement secure key management from the start.
actual_capabilities: Recommend the correct Stripe API for each integration pattern (Checkout Sessions, PaymentIntents, Setup Intents, Connect Accounts v2, Billing APIs)
Guide migration from deprecated Stripe APIs (Charges, Sources, Tokens, legacy Card Element) to current alternatives
Advise on Connect platform setup using Accounts v2 controller properties (losses, fees, dashboard, requirements)
Provide security best practices for API key management, restricted keys (RAKs), webhook signature verification, and OAuth state parameters
Explain Stripe Tax setup including automatic_tax configuration and jurisdiction registration
Direct embedded financial account integrations to the v2 Financial Accounts API
limitations: Does not execute or test Stripe API calls — provides guidance only
Does not store, manage, or rotate actual API keys
Cannot replace reading the official Stripe documentation for implementation details
Focused on integration architecture decisions rather than code-level debugging

use_cases.title: Choosing the right Stripe API for a new integration
use_cases.description: Determine whether to use Checkout Sessions, PaymentIntents, Setup Intents, or higher-level solutions like Invoicing or Payment Links for a given use case.
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers starting a new Stripe integration

use_cases.title: Migrating from deprecated Stripe APIs
use_cases.description: Replace legacy Charges API, Sources API, Tokens API, or Card Element usage with current best-practice alternatives.
use_cases.target_user: Developers maintaining or upgrading existing Stripe integrations

use_cases.title: Implementing secure Stripe key management
use_cases.description: Set up restricted API keys, webhook signature verification, IP allowlists, and OAuth state parameters to protect against key compromise and CSRF attacks.

prompt_templates.title: API selection for one-time payments
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm building a one-time payment flow for an e-commerce site. Should I use Checkout Sessions or PaymentIntents? What are the trade-offs?
prompt_templates.scenario: Choosing between Stripe integration surfaces for a new payment flow

prompt_templates.title: Connect platform setup guidance
prompt_templates.prompt: I'm building a marketplace platform with connected accounts. Should I use Accounts v2 or the legacy v1 Accounts API? Walk me through the controller properties I need to configure.
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up a Stripe Connect platform with marketplace functionality

prompt_templates.title: Subscription billing with dynamic payment methods
prompt_templates.prompt: I want to set up recurring subscriptions that automatically show the best payment methods to each customer. How do I configure Checkout Sessions for subscription mode without hardcoding payment_method_types?
prompt_templates.scenario: Implementing subscription billing with Stripe's dynamic payment methods

prompt_templates.title: Security audit of existing Stripe integration
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my Stripe integration for security issues. I'm using a secret key in my backend, I don't verify webhook signatures, and I'm not sure if I should switch to restricted API keys. What should I change?
prompt_templates.scenario: Performing a security review and hardening an existing Stripe integration

output_examples.input: Should I pass payment_method_types when creating a Checkout Session for a subscription?
output: No — never pass payment_method_types in a Stripe API call, with the sole exception of Terminal (in-person) integrations.

Omitting the parameter enables Dynamic Payment Methods: Stripe evaluates 100+ signals (currency, customer location, transaction amount, device) to automatically show the most relevant payment methods and rank them for maximum conversion.
Payment methods are managed from the Dashboard with no code changes required.
If you need to customize which methods appear, use payment_method_configurations or excluded_payment_method_types — never payment_method_types.

output_examples.input: What's the difference between destination charges and direct charges in Connect?
output: Destination charges — use when the platform accepts liability for negative balances. Funds route to the connected account via transfer_data.destination.
Direct charges — use when the platform wants Stripe to take risk on the connected account. The charge is created on the connected account directly.
Choose one charge type per integration — don't mix them.
Don't use the Charges API for Connect fund flows — use PaymentIntents or Checkout Sessions with transfer_data or on_behalf_of.

best_practices: Always recommend restricted API keys (RAK, rk_ prefix) over secret keys (sk_ prefix) and apply the principle of least privilege to each RAK
Never include payment_method_types in Stripe API calls (except Terminal) — let Stripe handle dynamic payment method selection automatically
Always verify webhook signatures using Stripe's signing secret, and allowlist Stripe's IP addresses on webhook endpoints for defense in depth
anti_patterns: Embedding secret or restricted API keys in client-side code, mobile apps, or source repositories
Hardcoding payment_method_types: ['card'] in Checkout Sessions or PaymentIntents, which blocks other eligible payment methods and reduces conversion
Using the legacy Charges API, Sources API, Tokens API, or Card Element for new integrations when current alternatives (Checkout Sessions, Setup Intents, Payment Element) are available

faq.question: What Stripe API version does this skill target?
faq.answer: The skill targets the latest Stripe API version, 2026-05-27.dahlia. It always recommends using the latest API version and SDK unless the user specifies otherwise.

faq.question: Should I use a secret key or a restricted API key?
faq.answer: Always recommend a restricted API key (RAK, rk_ prefix) over a secret key (sk_ prefix). RAKs have only the permissions you assign, so a compromised RAK can do far less damage than a compromised secret key.

faq.question: When should I use Checkout Sessions vs PaymentIntents?
faq.answer: Use Checkout Sessions for on-session payments (one-time or subscriptions) where Stripe handles taxes, discounts, and shipping. Use PaymentIntents for off-session payments or when the merchant needs to model checkout state independently.

faq.question: How do I secure Stripe webhook endpoints?
faq.answer: Always verify webhook signatures using Stripe's signing secret to confirm requests are genuinely from Stripe. For defense in depth, also allowlist Stripe's IP addresses on your webhook endpoint.

faq.question: What is the Accounts v2 API for Connect?
faq.answer: Accounts v2 (POST /v2/core/accounts) is Stripe's actively invested path for new Connect platforms. It uses controller properties (losses, fees, dashboard, requirements) instead of legacy account types like 'express', 'custom', or 'standard'.

faq.question: How do I enable dynamic payment methods?
faq.answer: Simply omit the payment_method_types parameter from Checkout Sessions, PaymentIntents, SetupIntents, and subscription creation calls. Stripe will automatically evaluate over 100 signals to show the most relevant payment methods for maximum conversion. The only exception is Terminal in-person payments.
